behavior_summary: U8P_BRIBOOK2::func0080 launches a Book::read process with a long embedded lore/quest text (the book contents) and then suspends the caller. This function's purpose is to present readable book text to the player when the book is used/read.
No branching, flag manipulation, or UI construction is done here; the function simply starts the read UI/process and yields.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BRIBOOK2::func0080 calls an external process Book::read(...) and stores the returned pid/handle in a local variable (temp).
Immediately after spawning Book::read the function issues a suspend, yielding until the spawned reading process finishes or yields.
open_questions: Exact in-game trigger: is U8P_BRIBOOK2::func0080 invoked by an item 'use' action, a world-object 'read' interaction, or both?
Is the embedded long text localized at runtime or baked into the script as a static string pointer?
Does the returned pid (local 'temp') get used later by other scripts or systems to track or resume the read process?

main_function.name: U8P_BRIBOOK2::func0080
main_function.purpose: Spawn the Book::read process to display a long book/lore text to the player and then suspend until that process yields/completes.
entry_conditions: Inferred: invoked when the player reads or interacts with this book object (use/read action on the item or world object).

function_map.name: U8P_BRIBOOK2::func0080
function_map.role: Starts the reading UI/process for this book; no branching or flag updates.

function_map.name: Book::read
function_map.role: External process provided by the engine that displays the provided text to the player; returns a pid/handle.

external_calls.purpose: Present the supplied long text string (book contents) in the reading UI as a separate process; returns a pid/handle captured by the caller.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_BRIBOOK2::func0080 launches a Book::read process with a long embedded lore/quest text (the book contents) and then suspends the caller. This function's purpose is to present readable book text to the player when the book is used/read.
